text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Delgado, Marcus T.
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]     AGE: 48     SEX: Male
ORDERING PROVIDER: Hematology/Oncology Attending
SERVICE: Inpatient Hematology/Oncology
CLINICAL INDICATION: New-onset leukocytosis, anemia, thrombocytopenia; peripheral blood blasts detected on automated differential. Evaluate for acute leukemia.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Right posterior iliac crest, core biopsy
  B. Left posterior iliac crest, core biopsy
  C. Bone marrow aspirate smears, right posterior iliac crest (10 smears submitted)
  D. Bone marrow aspirate, clot section
  E. Bone marrow aspirate, touch imprint preparations

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE REDACTED — TO BE INSERTED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
PERFORMED BY: Hematology/Oncology Fellow and Attending under procedural consent
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: Fresh, in 10% neutral buffered formalin (core biopsies); EDTA anticoagulated aspirate

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A (Right posterior iliac crest core biopsy): Received in formalin is a cylindrical core of bone and marrow tissue measuring 1.8 cm in length x 0.3 cm in greatest diameter. The specimen is tan-pink and firm with an intact cortical surface at one pole. The specimen is entirely submitted following decalcification (Surgipath Decalcifier I, 3-hour protocol), embedded in a single cassette, sectioned at 5 micrometers, and stained with H&E.

Specimen B (Left posterior iliac crest core biopsy): Received in formalin is a cylindrical core of bone and marrow tissue measuring 1.8 cm in length x 0.3 cm in greatest diameter. Tan-pink and firm, adequate trabecular bone present. Submitted in entirety following decalcification. Sectioned at 5 micrometers and stained with H&E.

Specimen C (Bone marrow aspirate smears): Ten aspirate smears received, labeled with patient identifier. Smears are moderately cellular with adequate spicules. Stained with Wright-Giemsa. Differential count performed on 500-cell manual differential from particle-rich areas.

Specimen D (Bone marrow aspirate clot section): Received is a formalin-fixed aspirate clot. A compact hypercellular clot aggregate is present, embedded entirely, sectioned at 5 micrometers, and stained with H&E.

Specimen E (Touch imprint preparations): Six air-dried touch imprint preparations stained with Wright-Giemsa. Adequately cellular.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

CORE BIOPSY (Specimens A and B):
Both core biopsies are adequate for interpretation. The marrow is markedly hypercellular, with an estimated overall cellularity of 90–95% (age-adjusted normal for a 48-year-old male: approximately 50–60%). The normal trilineage hematopoietic architecture is effaced. The marrow space is replaced by a monotonous, densely packed proliferation of immature myeloid blasts.

The neoplastic cells are medium-to-large in size, with scant-to-moderate pale cytoplasm and round-to-oval, irregular nuclei. Nuclear contours show occasional folding and lobation. Chromatin is finely dispersed and vesicular. Nucleoli are prominent, with one to three nucleoli per cell identifiable at high-power magnification. Cytoplasm is amphophilic to lightly basophilic; no granules are identified on H&E stain. No Auer rods are definitively identified on core biopsy sections (see aspirate differential below). Mitotic figures are readily identified.

Residue normal erythropoiesis is markedly suppressed; only rare erythroid island remnants are observed. Megakaryocytes are markedly reduced in number (fewer than 2 per core cross-section at low power). No normal granulopoiesis is identified. There is no evidence of significant stromal fibrosis on H&E.

No necrosis. No granulomas. No lymphoid aggregates.

BONE MARROW ASPIRATE SMEARS (Specimen C):
The aspirate smears are hypercellular and particle-rich with a near-total replacement of normal hematopoietic elements by blasts. Manual differential count (500 cells, particle-rich area):
  - Blasts: 78%
  - Promyelocytes: 2%
  - Myelocytes: 1%
  - Metamyelocytes/bands: <1%
  - Segmented neutrophils: 3%
  - Monocytes: 2%
  - Lymphocytes: 8%
  - Erythroid precursors: 5%
  - Other: 1%

Blast morphology: Medium-to-large cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Nuclei are round-to-irregular with fine, open chromatin and 1–3 prominent nucleoli. Cytoplasm is scant and pale basophilic. No granules detected in the majority of blasts on Wright-Giemsa; rare cells show very faint azurophilic granularity but Auer rods are NOT identified on aspirate smears after careful review. The morphologic appearance is most consistent with AML without maturation (FAB M1).

BONE MARROW ASPIRATE CLOT SECTION (Specimen D):
Consistent with core biopsy findings. Hypercellular clot aggregate with near-total replacement by immature blasts. No residual normal hematopoiesis identified. No fibrosis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RETICULIN STAIN (Silver impregnation, Gomori method):
Performed on both core biopsies (Specimens A and B). No significant increase in reticulin fiber density. Grade 0 fibrosis by WHO 2016 criteria (no reticulin fibers or rare individual fibers, no intersections). No collagen fibrosis (trichrome stain negative for collagen deposition).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on decalcified paraffin-embedded core biopsy, Specimen A):

All IHC performed with appropriate positive and negative controls.

  CD34 (QBEnd/10 clone):     POSITIVE — diffuse, strong membranous staining in the majority of blasts (estimated >70% of marrow elements). Markedly increased CD34-positive cells consistent with blast predominance.
  CD117 (c-Kit, polyclonal): POSITIVE — diffuse cytoplasmic and membranous staining, co-expressed with CD34.
  Myeloperoxidase (MPO):     POSITIVE — granular cytoplasmic positivity in a subset of blasts (approximately 40–50%); confirms myeloid lineage.
  CD33:                      POSITIVE — diffuse membranous staining (noted on flow cytometry correlation; IHC confirmatory).
  CD3 (polyclonal):          NEGATIVE — no aberrant T-lymphoid differentiation.
  CD20 (L26 clone):          NEGATIVE — no B-lymphoid component.
  CD64:                      NEGATIVE — no monocytic differentiation.
  TdT (terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase): NEGATIVE — excludes lymphoblastic leukemia/lymphoma.
  PAX5:                      NEGATIVE.
  Lysozyme:                  Focally positive in a minor subset; non-specific in this context.

Note: CD14 and CD56 IHC pending at time of this report (results to be reported as addendum).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (Performed on bone marrow aspirate, Specimen C — multiparameter 10-color panel):
Performed and reported separately under case [FLOW CYTOMETRY CASE NUMBER REDACTED]. Summary for correlation:
  Blast gate (CD45-dim, low SSC): constitutes approximately 75–80% of analyzed events.
  Positive: CD34, CD117 (bright), CD13, CD33, HLA-DR, MPO (cytoplasmic)
  Negative: CD14, CD64 (monocytic markers negative), CD19, CD22, CD79a (B-lineage negative), CD3, CD5, CD7 (T-lineage negative), TdT (negative)
  CD56: negative (no aberrant NK-cell marker expression)
  Immunophenotype is consistent with acute myeloid leukemia without monocytic or B/T-lymphoid differentiation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CYTOGENETICS (Conventional G-banded karyotype; performed on bone marrow aspirate, Specimen C — reported separately under Cytogenetics case [REDACTED]):
Result: 46,XY [20/20 metaphases analyzed]
Interpretation: Normal male karyotype. No structural or numerical chromosomal abnormalities identified. No evidence of translocation t(8;21), inv(16)/t(16;16), t(15;17), 11q23/KMT2A rearrangement, complex karyotype, monosomy 5, del(5q), monosomy 7, del(7q), or trisomy 8.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MOLECULAR STUDIES (Reported separately; results pending at time of initial sign-out — ADDENDUM to follow):
  - FLT3-ITD mutation analysis (PCR-based fragment analysis): PENDING
  - NPM1 mutation (exon 12): PENDING
  - CEBPA mutation (full coding sequence): PENDING
  - Comprehensive myeloid NGS panel (283-gene panel, next-generation sequencing): PENDING
  - FISH panel for AML-associated rearrangements [t(8;21)/RUNX1-RUNX1T1, inv(16)/CBFB-MYH11, t(15;17)/PML-RARA, KMT2A breakapart]: PENDING

  NOTE: Molecular results will be issued as a separate addendum and/or as independent laboratory reports when finalized. Clinical correlation and final risk stratification should await complete molecular data.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

SPECIMENS A AND B — BILATERAL POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST, CORE BIOPSIES:
  ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA, involving bone marrow
  - Markedly hypercellular marrow (90–95% cellularity) with near-total replacement by myeloid blasts
  - Blast percentage by morphology (aspirate differential): 78%
  - Immunophenotype consistent with AML without maturation (FAB M1)
  - No reticulin fibrosis (Grade 0 by WHO)
  - Normal male karyotype: 46,XY
  - Molecular studies PENDING (see above)

SPECIMEN C — BONE MARROW ASPIRATE SMEARS:
  CONSISTENT WITH ABOVE; blasts 78% of nucleated cells

SPECIMEN D — BONE MARROW ASPIRATE CLOT SECTION:
  CONSISTENT WITH ABOVE

SPECIMEN E — TOUCH IMPRINT PREPARATIONS:
  CONSISTENT WITH ABOVE; no Auer rods identified

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The bone marrow biopsy and aspirate findings are diagnostic of acute myeloid leukemia (AML). The marrow demonstrates markedly hypercellular architecture with near-complete obliteration of normal trilineage hematopoiesis and replacement by a blast population accounting for 78% of nucleated marrow cells (WHO 2022 threshold for AML diagnosis: ≥20% blasts). The immunophenotypic profile (CD34+, CD117+, MPO+, CD13+, CD33+, HLA-DR+; negative for monocytic, T-, and B-lymphoid markers; TdT negative) is consistent with AML without maturation. The cytogenetic result reveals a normal diploid karyotype (46,XY), which is provisionally assigned intermediate risk pending molecular mutation profiling.

Molecular studies, including FLT3-ITD/TKD, NPM1, CEBPA, IDH1/2, and next-generation sequencing, are submitted and results are pending. Final WHO 2022 classification and ELN 2022 risk stratification should be deferred until molecular data are available. Correlation with peripheral blood counts, flow cytometry report, and clinical findings is recommended.

Signed by:
[Attending Hematopathologist — Signature and credentials redacted per de-identification protocol]
Hematopathology Division, Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

Report Status: FINAL (molecular addendum pending)
